label: Clear task with steps and example
prompt: Explain how sleep supports memory consolidation. Give three numbered steps and one concrete example a student could use when revising for an exam.

label: Comparison with output shape
prompt: Compare spaced practice and cramming for long-term retention. End with a short weekly revision plan in bullet points.

label: Neutral, evidence-framed health question
prompt: Summarize what systematic reviews typically report about caffeine and sleep quality. Use cautious language and note at least one limitation of the evidence.

label: Definition plus constraints
prompt: Define working memory in plain language. Use exactly two short paragraphs and highlight one common misconception.

label: Structured list request
prompt: List five habits that support cognitive health in older adults. For each habit, add one sentence explaining why it may help.

label: Spam / shouty
prompt: BUY NOW!!! AMAZING OFFER!!! LIMITED TIME!!!

label: No clear action
prompt: The mitochondria is the powerhouse of the cell. It produces ATP for the cell to use.

label: Very long single paragraph
prompt: Why is sleep important for memory? Why is sleep important for memory? Why is sleep important for memory? Explain in detail with examples and mention learning, brain, health, and consolidation themes repeated for stress testing.

label: Contradiction-prone request
prompt: Write two sentences: first say exercise always improves sleep, then say exercise never improves sleep.

label: Non-English mixed snippet
prompt: Explain sleep 睡眠 memory — answer in English only, two sentences.

label: Role-play boundary
prompt: You are a doctor giving a diagnosis for my headache. List three possible causes without asking follow-up questions.

label: Numeric precision ask
prompt: Give a rough range (hours) for recommended sleep for adults according to common public health guidance, and cite that it is a guideline not personal medical advice.
